title: leucas cephalotes
intro: Leucas cephalotes, also known as the Wild Basil or Elephant's Head, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. Leucas cephalotes is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive problems.
